description: Project Management - Replace ProjectSelectionPage with a full ProjectManagementPage that lets users register (adopt/create/maskor-managed), rename, locate moved vaults, and deregister projects without typing filesystem paths. Backed by a directory-browse endpoint, settings file, trash helper, and wired-up PATCH rename route.

userStories.title: Settings service with maskor-managed root key
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a persisted settings service so the maskor-managed root can be configured and read across the app.
acceptanceCriteria: New settings service reads/writes `~/.config/maskor/settings.json` (path resolved via `os.homedir()`)
Schema includes `maskorManagedRoot: string` with OS-aware defaults: `~/Documents/Maskor/` on macOS/Linux, `%USERPROFILE%\Documents\Maskor\` on Windows
Missing keys fall back to defaults without error
An unparsable file is treated as empty and a warning string is returned alongside the settings object
File is created on first write only (not on read)
Unit tests cover: defaults when file missing, missing-key fallback, unparsable-file returns warning + defaults, first-write creates file
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: GET /fs/list directory-browse endpoint
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a backend endpoint that lists directory contents so the frontend folder picker can navigate the host filesystem without a native shell.
acceptanceCriteria: `GET /fs/list?path=<absolute>` returns `{ path, parent, entries: [{ name, kind: "file" | "directory", hidden, hasMaskorManifest, hasObsidianDir }] }`
`hasMaskorManifest` is true when the directory contains `.maskor/project.json`; `hasObsidianDir` is true when it contains `.obsidian/` — both only computed for `kind === "directory"`
`hidden` is true for entries whose name starts with `.`
Rejects relative paths with 400
Returns 404 if the path does not exist
Returns 403 if the path exists but is not readable
Endpoint is bound to localhost only (verify existing server config matches)
Unit tests cover: entry classification, hidden flag, missing path (404), permission denied (403), relative path (400)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: POST /projects accepts mode and reuses manifest UUID on adopt
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the registration endpoint to accept `mode: "adopt" | "create"` and reuse an existing manifest UUID on adopt, closing the re-register gap.
acceptanceCriteria: `POST /projects` body accepts `mode: "adopt" | "create"`; existing callers updated
`mode: "adopt"`: validates that the path exists; if `.maskor/project.json` is present, the registry row UUID equals the manifest UUID and manifest name is used (overrideable by request body)
`mode: "adopt"` without manifest: assigns a new UUID
`mode: "create"`: creates directory if missing via `mkdir -p`, assigns new UUID, writes manifest
`vaultPath` uniqueness in the registry is preserved; conflict returns 409
Existing storage-service note that manifests cannot be reused on re-register is removed
Unit/integration tests cover: adopt-with-manifest reuses UUID, adopt-without-manifest assigns new UUID, create-when-missing creates dir, vaultPath conflict rejected
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Vault skeleton init on mode: create
userStories.description: As a user creating a new project, I expect Maskor to set up the vault structure so the project is immediately usable.
acceptanceCriteria: On `mode: "create"`, Maskor writes `.maskor/`, `.maskor/project.json`, and an empty `aspects/` directory inside the vault path
Init is idempotent: re-running on an already-initialized folder does not error or overwrite existing manifest
Other vault structure (arcs config, vault DB) is still created lazily by its owning feature — no regressions
Unit tests cover: empty-folder init creates expected structure, existing-skeleton is a no-op, manifest contents are correct
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Trash helper using trash npm package
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a single helper that moves a path to OS trash so deregister-with-delete prefers reversible deletion.
acceptanceCriteria: `trash` added as a backend dependency
Helper `moveToTrashOrDelete(absolutePath): Promise<{ method: "trash" | "hard-delete" }>` tries trash first, falls back to hard delete (`fs.rm`) if trash throws
Unit tests mock `trash` and verify: trash succeeds (method is `"trash"`), trash throws (method is `"hard-delete"` and hard delete runs)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: PATCH /projects/:projectId for rename
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to wire ProjectUpdateSchema to an HTTP route so project rename works end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: `PATCH /projects/:projectId` accepts `{ name: string }` validated by the existing `ProjectUpdateSchema`
Updates the registry row's name and the `name` field in `.maskor/project.json`
Never moves or renames the on-disk folder — verified by test
Returns 404 when project is not found
Returns the updated project record on success
Unit/integration tests cover: success updates registry + manifest, missing project returns 404, folder name is unchanged after rename
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: FolderPicker reusable UI component
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse my filesystem inside Maskor and pick a folder so I never have to type an absolute path.
acceptanceCriteria: Reusable `<FolderPicker>` component opens at the user's home directory by default (via `GET /fs/list?path=<home>`)
Up button navigates to parent directory; clicking a directory navigates into it; clicking a file does nothing (files are visible but disabled)
Address bar shows the current absolute path; user can edit it and press Enter to navigate
"Show hidden" toggle (off by default) reveals entries whose `hidden` flag is true
Each directory row badges `.maskor` (Maskor project) when `hasMaskorManifest` is true and `.obsidian` (Obsidian vault) when `hasObsidianDir` is true
"Choose this folder" button calls an `onSelect(path: string)` callback with the current path
Loading state shown while fetch is in flight; permission-error state shown inline (not as fatal error); empty-directory state shown when entries array is empty
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: ProjectManagementPage replaces ProjectSelectionPage
userStories.description: As a user, I want a single page that lists all my registered projects so I can act on them in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: `ProjectSelectionPage` is renamed to `ProjectManagementPage` and all references (router, links, imports) are updated
Page lists registered projects (name + vault path), one row per project in an unordered list

Each row exposes: Open, Rename, Locate vault… (shown instead of Open when vaultPath is missing on disk), Deregister — as placeholder buttons (wired to console.log or no-op; flows added in later stories)
At page load, each row checks whether `vaultPath` exists on disk (a per-row call to `GET /fs/list` or equivalent); Locate vault… replaces Open when path is missing
Page has three sections in this order: project list (top), "Register project" (middle, three entry-point cards as placeholders), "Settings" (bottom, placeholder)
No sort, search, or filter affordances
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Adopt-existing registration flow
userStories.description: As a user, I want to point Maskor at an existing folder so I can use Maskor with an Obsidian vault or prior Maskor project without losing my UUID.
acceptanceCriteria: "Adopt existing folder" card on ProjectManagementPage opens the FolderPicker (US-007)

After picking, a confirmation step shows: project name (manifest name if present, else folder basename, editable) and detected folder kind: Maskor project (manifest present), Obsidian vault, writing folder (markdown files), empty, or other
When folder contains non-markdown files unrelated to writing, a warning shows the non-markdown file count
Submitting calls `POST /projects` with `mode: "adopt"` and the picked path
On success, the new project appears in the list; the row's UUID equals the manifest UUID when `.maskor/project.json` was present (verified by inspecting the returned project)
No markdown content is auto-imported — fragment list for the new project is empty after adoption
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Create-at-custom-path registration flow
userStories.description: As a user, I want to pick or type any path on my disk and have Maskor initialize a project there.
acceptanceCriteria: "Create new project" card opens the FolderPicker; user can also type a path that does not yet exist in the address bar
If the path does not exist, Maskor creates it via `mkdir -p` (handled by `POST /projects` with `mode: "create"`)
If path exists and is non-empty with unrelated content, the same warning surface as adopt-flow is shown
Submitting calls `POST /projects` with `mode: "create"` and the chosen path
After submission, the new project's vault folder contains `.maskor/project.json` and an empty `aspects/` directory
New project appears in the list on success
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Maskor-managed registration flow
userStories.description: As a user who doesn't want to think about filesystem paths, I want to type a name and have Maskor create the project folder for me under a managed root.
acceptanceCriteria: "Use Maskor-managed folder" card shows only a name input — no folder picker
Slug derivation: lowercase, ASCII-fold accented chars, replace non-alphanumerics with `-`, collapse repeated `-`, strip leading/trailing `-`; empty result falls back to `project`
On slug collision within the managed root, silently suffix `-2`, `-3`, …
Confirmation step shows the resolved absolute path (e.g. `Project will be created at ~/Documents/Maskor/my-novel-2/`) before commit
If the configured managed root does not exist on disk, Maskor `mkdir -p`s it before creating the project folder (no user prompt)
Submitting calls `POST /projects` with `mode: "create"` and the resolved absolute path
Unit tests cover slug derivation: non-ASCII input, leading numbers, empty-after-strip (falls back to `project`), collision (suffix -2)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Rename action on each project row
userStories.description: As a user, I want to rename a project from the management page so its display name stays meaningful over time.
acceptanceCriteria: Row-level "Rename" action opens an inline editor or modal seeded with the current project name
Submitting calls `PATCH /projects/:projectId` with `{ name }` (US-006)
On success, the row updates to show the new name without a full page reload
The on-disk folder name is unchanged after rename
The manifest on disk (`.maskor/project.json`) reflects the new name after rename
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Locate vault action for moved/missing paths
userStories.description: As a user whose vault folder moved on disk, I want to re-point its registry entry so I can keep using the project.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking "Locate vault…" on a row with a missing path opens the FolderPicker
New backend endpoint `PATCH /projects/:projectId/vault-path` accepts `{ newPath: string, forceOverride?: boolean }` and updates the registry row's `vaultPath`
If the picked folder contains `.maskor/project.json` whose UUID matches the row's UUID, re-point succeeds silently

If the picked folder contains `.maskor/project.json` whose UUID differs, a confirmation dialog appears: "This folder belongs to a different Maskor project. Re-point anyway?"; on confirm (`forceOverride: true`), row UUID is preserved and manifest is rewritten with the row's UUID
If `vaultPath` uniqueness would be violated by the new path, the action errors with a clear message
On success, the row's vault path updates and the Open button replaces Locate vault…
Tests cover: simple re-point, UUID-conflict prompt + rewrite, uniqueness violation
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Deregister with optional file deletion
userStories.description: As a user, I want to deregister a project, with an optional irreversible step to also delete its files.
acceptanceCriteria: Row-level "Deregister" opens a confirmation dialog
Dialog has an "Also delete the vault folder from disk" checkbox, unchecked by default
When checkbox is checked, a text input appears requiring the user to type the project name exactly; confirm button stays disabled until names match

`DELETE /projects/:projectId` accepts `{ deleteFiles: boolean }` in the request body; when `deleteFiles` is true, calls `moveToTrashOrDelete` helper (US-005) on the vault path before removing the registry row
Confirmation copy distinguishes "moved to Trash" vs "permanently deleted" based on platform trash availability (helper returns which method ran)
Without checkbox: registry row removed, vault files untouched
With checkbox: vault trashed (preferred) or hard-deleted, then registry row removed
Tests cover: deregister-only (files untouched), deregister-with-delete-trash, deregister-with-delete-hard-fallback
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Settings section on the management page
userStories.description: As a user, I want to change where Maskor stores managed projects so I can keep them somewhere other than ~/Documents/Maskor/.
acceptanceCriteria: "Settings" section at the bottom of ProjectManagementPage exposes a single field: "Where to keep Maskor-managed projects" (text input + "Browse…" button that opens FolderPicker)

A settings endpoint `GET /settings` returns current settings (including `maskorManagedRoot` with default applied); `PATCH /settings` accepts `{ maskorManagedRoot: string }` and writes to `~/.config/maskor/settings.json`
Submitting writes the value via the settings endpoint; on success the input updates to reflect the saved value
Changing the value does not move or re-link existing maskor-managed projects (verified by test)
If the settings file was unparsable, the warning from the settings service is shown inline in this section
Tests cover: read default, write updates file, existing projects unaffected, unparsable file surfaces warning
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
